title: README references non-existent `dashboard_info` interface; should be `catalogue`
summary: charm/README.md (Usage section) instructs users to relate the charm to 'any charms implementing the providing side of the `dashboard_info` interface'. The charm does not use a `dashboard_info` interface anywhere; the relation interface for catalogue entries is `catalogue` (provides: catalogue / requires: catalogue-item, both interface: catalogue per charmcraft.yaml). `dashboard_info` is Grafana's interface and appears to be a copy-paste error. The Usage line should reference the `catalogue` interface instead.
why_good_first_issue: Single-line documentation fix with an unambiguous correct value. No code, no architecture knowledge, and no build/test run required. The wrong term is verifiable against charmcraft.yaml in seconds, and 'done' is obvious: replace `dashboard_info` with `catalogue`.
repro_steps: On the cos VM: git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/catalogue-k8s-operator ~/work/catalogue-k8s-operator; cd ~/work/catalogue-k8s-operator; grep -rn 'dashboard_info' . --include='*.md' --include='*.py' --include='*.yaml'  (only hit: charm/README.md:14); grep -n 'interface:' charm/charmcraft.yaml  (shows provides/requires use 'interface: catalogue', never dashboard_info).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: charm/README.md:14 says 'charms implementing the providing side of the `dashboard_info` interface', but `dashboard_info` occurs nowhere else in the repo; charmcraft.yaml:59-72 shows the relevant interface is `catalogue` (provides catalogue / requires catalogue-item, both interface: catalogue). So the README names the wrong interface.
fix_hint: In charm/README.md line 14, replace `dashboard_info` with `catalogue` (the interface used by the `catalogue` provider / `catalogue-item` requirer). Optionally clarify that providers relate over the `catalogue` relation endpoint.
notes: Verified against repo at commit 13071f7 (branch main, 2026-06-16). Pure docs change; no LIBPATCH bump and no tests affected. Lint and unit envs are green on this commit (10 unit tests pass).

title: Remove dead `_is_valid_unit_address` method (and stale `unit_address` docstring) in catalogue v1 lib
summary: In charm/lib/charms/catalogue_k8s/v1/catalogue.py, CatalogueConsumer defines a private helper `_is_valid_unit_address` (lines 148-163) that is never called anywhere in the library or charm. It is a leftover from the v0 library, where it IS used by `unit_address` (v0 line 115). In v1, `unit_address` was simplified to just return `self._item.url` (or ''), so the IP-validation helper is dead code. The v1 `unit_address` docstring (lines 139-146) still claims it returns a unit address that 'Requires ingress to be connected', which no longer matches the trivial body.
why_good_first_issue: Small, self-contained cleanup confined to one file. The 'unused' claim is easy to verify with grep and is even visible in the unit-test coverage report (the method's lines show as uncovered). No behavior change, low risk, and a clear definition of done: delete the unused method and tidy the misleading docstring.
repro_steps: On the cos VM: cd ~/work/catalogue-k8s-operator; grep -rn '_is_valid_unit_address' charm/  (defined at v1/catalogue.py:148 and v0/catalogue.py:120; CALLED only at v0/catalogue.py:115; no call site in v1 or in src/charm.py); export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e unit  (10 passed; coverage report lists v1/catalogue.py 'Missing' lines including 158-163 and 144-146).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection + unit: `_is_valid_unit_address` is defined at charm/lib/charms/catalogue_k8s/v1/catalogue.py:148 but has no caller in v1 or in src/charm.py (grep shows it is only invoked in the v0 lib at line 115). The unit run (10 passed) coverage report flags v1/catalogue.py lines 158-163 (the method body) and 144-146 (unit_address body) as Missing/never-executed, corroborating that the method is dead code.
fix_hint: Delete the unused `_is_valid_unit_address` method from CatalogueConsumer in the v1 library, and update the `unit_address` docstring to reflect that it now simply returns the item's URL. Because this edits a published charm lib, bump LIBPATCH (currently 3) in v1/catalogue.py per the repo's static-analysis check in charm/tox.ini, which fails if a lib changes without a LIBPATCH/LIBAPI bump.
notes: Verified against repo at commit 13071f7 (branch main, 2026-06-16). Slightly more involved than a pure docs fix only because the static env enforces a LIBPATCH bump for any lib change; otherwise trivial. unit and lint envs are green on this commit before the change.
